content:
Claude Code

21 min read
Just now

--

Disclosure: I use GPT search to collection facts. The entire article is drafted by me.
There’s a certain ritual that happens every time a new AI tool gains traction.
Someone writes a thread. “27 tips for getting the most out of [tool].” The tips are good. People save the thread. Some actually read it. A few implement it. And then, a few weeks later, the same people end up in the same place — things sort of work, until they don’t, and nobody can explain exactly why.
Claude Code has its own version of this. Rahul’s widely shared thread covering 27 tips — from /init and /compact to Plan Mode, Subagents, Hooks, Worktrees, DevTools, and Loop — is genuinely useful. Most of the tips are grounded. Try them in sequence, and you'll see results immediately.
But here’s what the tips list won’t tell you: most of them are solving the same five problems.
And if you understand those five problems, you stop treating Claude Code like a bag of clever tricks and start treating it like what it actually is — a system that’s trying to make AI participation in software engineering structurally sound.
This article isn’t a command walkthrough. It’s a structural autopsy. By the end, you should be able to look at any new Claude Code feature and immediately understand which of the five…
